summary: Re-ran 9 CORS tests after main agent applied both Option A (.env CORS_ORIGINS append 4 farforevent variants) and Option B (server.py merge refactor: env origins UNIONED with default_origins, ordered de-dup). All 9/9 tests PASS. Backend healthy. Manual curl OPTIONS preflight smoke confirmed: all 4 new farforevent.com.ua origins return 200 with proper ACAO echo + ACAC=true; existing prod origins (rentalhub.farforrent.com.ua, item-photos.preview.emergentagent.com) still 200; disallowed evil-domain.example.com returns 400 with NO ACAO echo. Regression check passed: login as vitokdrako@gmail.com/test123 → /api/event/cabinet/profile returns matching email (data-leak fix from iteration_1 still intact). Code path inspection confirms: even if CORS_ORIGINS env was empty, the else-branch (lines 70-72) would fall through to default_origins which already contains all 4 farforevent variants — so future additions to default_origins will propagate regardless of .env state.

minor.issue: Starlette CORSMiddleware returns 400 for preflight with disallowed origin and still emits Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true (harmless: no ACAO so browser blocks; spec-compliant). Not a bug, just noting behavior.

action_items: No code action needed on backend CORS — fix is complete and verified.
User-side / VPS: DNS A-record for farforevent.com.ua → VPS IP, certbot for HTTPS, Nginx vhost. Tracked in /app/deploy/SETUP_NEW_DOMAIN_farforevent.md (out of scope for code testing).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py CORS merge logic (lines 62-69) is clean: ordered de-dup via dict.fromkeys preserves env precedence while guaranteeing defaults are never silently dropped — good fix.
server.py is 188 lines but the route-include block (90-168) is repetitive — consider grouping into a registry list in future; not blocking.
Long single-line import on server.py line 15 (60+ modules) hurts readability; consider splitting per-package. Not blocking.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CORS for farforevent.com.ua is fully fixed at the code level (server.py merge logic) + .env level. All 9 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_cors_new_domain.py pass. The remaining ERR_TUNNEL_CONNECTION_FAILED in browser is PURELY a DNS/VPS issue (NXDOMAIN on farforevent.com.ua) — not in scope. If user reports it still broken in browser after DNS is configured, check Nginx vhost on VPS, not backend code.
rca of the issue: Iteration_2 root cause: when CORS_ORIGINS env was present, the elif-branch in server.py REPLACED default_origins instead of merging — so the 4 newly added farforevent entries in default_origins had no runtime effect because .env still listed only the old origins. Fix applied by main agent in iteration_3: (a) refactored elif-branch to UNION env_origins with default_origins via dict.fromkeys ordered de-dup (b) appended 4 farforevent variants to .env CORS_ORIGINS as a belt-and-suspenders safeguard. Both fixes independently sufficient; together they make future additions to default_origins propagate even if ops forget to update .env.
